'''Ben Murdoch-Masila''' (born 7 February 1991) is a retired [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} and {{rlp|lk}} for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

He played for the [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the NRL, and the [[Salford Red Devils]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Murdoch-Masila was born in [[Otahuhu, New Zealand]] and is of Tongan descent.{{cn|date=March 2024}}

==Playing career==
Murdoch-Masila played junior football with Otahuhu Black. He undertook a scholarship with Wests Tigers at Keebra Park High School and was a member of the team that won the 2009 [[ARL Schoolboy Cup|Arrive Alive Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Murdoch-Masila|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display-nyc&id=2287&team=Wests+Tigers-NYC&ben-murdoch-masila|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120918011621/http://www.weststigers.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display-nyc&id=2287&team=Wests+Tigers-NYC&ben-murdoch-masila|url-status=dead|archive-date=18 September 2012|access-date=29 September 2010|website=weststigers.com.au}}</ref> Murdoch-Masila weighed 150 kg when he arrived at Keebra Park, dropping 40 kg before he joined the Wests Tigers [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|title=Weight loss turns Ben into big winner|author=Jake Dew|url=http://www.goldcoast.com.au/article/2009/08/19/109695_sport.html|access-date=29 September 2009|website=goldcoast.com.au|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120406063530/http://www.goldcoast.com.au/article/2009/08/19/109695_sport.html|archive-date=6 April 2012}}</ref>

Murdoch-Masila made his NRL debut in the first round of the 2010 NRL semi finals against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>[http://www.sportsnewsfirst.com.au/articles/2010/09/11/roosters-snatch-epic-finals-win/]  {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110706112213/http://www.sportsnewsfirst.com.au/articles/2010/09/11/roosters-snatch-epic-finals-win/|date=6 July 2011}}</ref> He is one of very few players to have made his first appearance in a finals match.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=Sydney Morning Herald|title=Rookie proves he's worth his salt in 100-minute blockbuster|author=Glenn Jackson|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/rookie-proves-hes-worth-his-salt-in-100minute-blockbuster-20100912-1572r.html|access-date=29 September 2010|date=13 September 2010| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100915040459/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/rookie-proves-hes-worth-his-salt-in-100minute-blockbuster-20100912-1572r.html| archive-date= 15 September 2010| url-status= live}}</ref>

In 2011, Murdoch-Masila made a further seven appearances, and may have made more if not for injuries. At the end of the season he was selected for the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41423 Junior Kiwis dominated by NYC Grand Finalists] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111007200909/http://rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41423 |date=7 October 2011}} ''rleague.com'', 5 October 2011</ref>

A regular on the bench in 2012, Murdoch-Masila played in 20 games for the season and scored five tries, including a double against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. He also signed an extension to stay with the club until the end of 2015, with coach [[Tim Sheens]] saying, "There is no doubt Ben has enormous talent and potential. He is a strike weapon and can become a top line player of the game with the right kind of commitment to his training regime."<ref>{{cite web|work=weststigers.com.au|title=Ben Murdoch-Masila signs on until the end of 2015|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Ben-MurdochMasila-signs-on-until-the-end-of-2015/63031|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130101144002/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Ben-MurdochMasila-signs-on-until-the-end-of-2015/63031|url-status=dead|archive-date=1 January 2013|access-date=19 September 2012|date=31 August 2012}}</ref>

Missing only one game, Murdoch-Masila was one of the most regular first grade players for [[Wests Tigers]] in 2013. He started the season playing from the reserve bench, but started in the back row in the last five games of the season. In April, Murdoch-Masila made his international debut, playing in the Pacific Rugby League International for the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tongan]] side that thrashed [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] 36–4.<ref>{{cite news|author=Adrian Warren|work=Fox Sports|title=Tonga smashes Samoa in a chaotic league Test shutout at Penrith's Centrebet Stadium|url= http://www.foxsports.com.au/league/tonga-smashes-samoa-in-a-chaotic-league-test-shutout/story-e6frf3ou-1226625107999|access-date=30 November 2013|date=20 April 2013}}</ref> He was chosen to represent Tonga in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]], but a leg injury in his first hit-up of the tournament saw him take no further part.<ref>{{cite news|author=Steve Mascord|work=Border Mail|title=RLWC: Scotland upset Tonga with late try|url=http://www.bordermail.com.au/story/1875173/rlwc-scotland-upset-tonga-with-late-try/?cs=2382|access-date=31 October 2013|date=30 October 2013}}</ref>

On 30 April 2014, Murdoch-Masila signed a {{frac|1|1|2}}-year contract effective immediately with the [[Penrith Panthers]], after being released from the final year and a half of his Wests Tigers contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/panthers-sign-ben-murdoch-masila-13172/#ki0gK9oxXkL4RmgW.97|title=Panthers sign Ben Murdoch-Masila | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-04-30|access-date=2015-10-01}}</ref>
Murdoch-Masila was a cult hero at Warrington Wolves, and has his own terrace song;
”1,2,3,4 Party on, Ben Murdoch-Masila,
Party on - he's a Tongan hero,
Party on, he never gives the ball away-ay”.
He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>
In round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he made his club debut for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in their 19–6 victory over the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].  He made a total of 11 appearances for the New Zealand club as they finished 15th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/warriors/warriors-farewell-15-players-after-return-to-new-zealand/news-story/10c5db674841c74a716fce5ece20e047/|title=Warriors farewell 15 players after return to New Zealand|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2023 ===
On 1 January 2023, Murdoch-Masila signed a two-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]. During round 2 of [[2023 NRL season]] against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], Murdoch-Masila made his club debut for the St. George Illawarra Dragons in the team's first win of the season against the Gold Coast. On 29 June 2023, Murdoch-Masila played his 100th game against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] with the club losing 16–52 in their biggest ever loss against their arch rivals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-cronulla-sharks-v-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-blog-updates-video-stats-ins-and-outs-injuries-ben-hunt/news-story/3cedb01ddff778981ebd7c6559648643/|title=Dragons cop worst ever beatdown from brutal Sharks as Hynes sends Origin message — 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
Murdoch-Masila played a total of 21 games for the club in the [[2023 NRL season]] as they finished 16th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-st-george-illawarra-dragons-end-of-season-review-brutal-review-rugby-league-news-ben-hunt-shane-flanagan/news-story/876c40f7fc8f8cf09cf2c720499f7db2/|title=Hunt call that could define Flanagan era; glaring hole that must be filled: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
Murdoch-Masila played 14 games for St. George Illawarra in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/the-mole-st-george-illawarra-dragons-2024-what-went-wrong-season-review-20240910-p5k9kr.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: 'Bullies' exposed as big-money Dragons forward called out|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Murdoch-Masila signed on a development contract for the 2025 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2024-12-02 |title=Dragons confirm development list for 2025 |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/dragons-confirm-development-list-for-2025-217544/ |access-date=2025-03-03 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref> On 17 September, Murdoch-Masila confirmed he would retire from the NRL at the conclusion of the NRL season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pryde |first=Scott |date=2025-09-17 |title=12 players to exit Dragons as Flanagan's rebuild continues |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/12-players-to-exit-dragons-as-flanagans-rebuild-continues-226945/ |access-date=2025-09-17 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>
On 28 September, he played in St. George Illawarra's 30-12 NSW Cup Grand Final loss to [[New Zealand Warriors|New Zealand]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2025/09/28/boyd-brilliance-steers-warriors-to-first-ever-nsw-cup/|title=Boyd brilliance steers Warriors to first ever NSW Cup|website=www.nswrl.com.au}}</ref>

==International career==
On 2 May 2015, Murdoch-Masila played for Tonga in their [[Polynesian Cup]] clash with Pacific rivals Samoa. During the 2015 season, he did not play any NRL games for the Panthers and was not re-signed beyond 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/panthers-farewell-departing-players-19173/#fq7yWMWKmadqhaEH.97|title=Panthers farewell departing players | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=2015-10-01}}</ref> On 17 October 2015, Murdoch-Masila played for Tonga in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] match against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

He played in every match of Tonga's historic charge to the semi-finals of the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]], and set up a vital try in their shock win over [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the pool stage of the tournament.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/11/11/tonga-stun-new-zealand-to-top-pool-b/|title = Tonga stun New Zealand to top Pool B|date = 11 November 2017}}</ref>
